full_text:
Received a small tumor piece. Tumor tissue was cut and dissociated using Dispase: 9.7 ml DMEM/F12+++ (DMEM/F12 supplemented with 1X glutamax, 10 mM HEPES, and 1% pen/step) + 200 µl Dispase + 100 µl DNAse. Incubated at 37C rotating for 30 min. Filtered through 70 µm cell strainer, RBC lysis with 2 ml for 5 min + 10 ml DMEM/F12+++. During RBC lysis incubation, transfered the cell suspension to a clean 50 ml falcon because the tube contained so much fat along the walls. Resuspended the pellet in 1 ml for counting.

Cell counts:

Cell count/ml 1):

total: 2.28 E6

live: 0.255 E6

viability: 11.2 %

size: 9.4 um

Cell count /ml 2):

total: 1.93 E6

live: 0.197 E6

viability: 10.2 %

size: 8.5 um

Average cell count/ml: 1.785 E6 cells/ml

Total cell count: 1.785 E6 cells

Total volume: 1 mL

Long-term organoid establishment

The initial plan was to seed two wells with 1.6 x 10⁵ cells per well, using 8 drops per well. However, after counting, the cells did not pellet, despite multiple attempts. Because of that, decided to just plate everything.

Added 130 µl of Cultrex solution to the cells and then distributed 7 drops of 20 µl between two wells: 4 for M1 and 3 for M2.

After 30 min, added warmed M1 and M2 medias to the cells.

Overall, the viability was so low that decided that it is okay to not freeze anything and just seed everything. The live cell count was already low, and freezing would have likely resulted in even fewer viable cells.
